contentBlocks.original:
नवदुर्गास्तोत्रम्
देवी शैलपुत्री ।
वन्दे वाञ्छितलाभाय चन्द्रार्धकृतशेखराम् ।
वृषारूढां शूलधरां शैलपुत्रीं यशस्विनीम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
navadurgāstotram
devī śailaputrī .
vande vāñchitalābhāya candrārdhakṛtaśekharām .
vṛṣārūḍhāṃ śūladharāṃ śailaputrīṃ yaśasvinīm ..
contentBlocks.translation: Navadurgā Stotram — I worship the illustrious Śailaputrī (daughter of the mountain — the first of the nine Durgās) for the attainment of all that is desired (vande vāñchitalābhāya): she who wears the half-crescent moon as her crown (candrārdhakṛtaśēkharā), who rides upon the bull (vṛṣārūḍhā), holds the trident (śūladharā) — Śailaputrī, glorious and adorned.
contentBlocks.meanings: नवदुर्गास्तोत्रम् (navadurgāstotram) – hymn to the nine Durgas (नवदुर्गा:nine Durgas | स्तोत्रम्:hymn); देवी (devī) – goddess; शैलपुत्री (śailaputrī) – daughter of the mountain; वन्दे (vande) – I salute; वाञ्छितलाभाय (vāñchitalābhāya) – for desired gain (वाञ्छित:desired | लाभाय:for gain); चन्द्रार्धकृतशेखराम् (candrārdhakṛtaśekharām) – crowned with half-moon (चन्द्रार्ध:half-moon | कृत:made | शेखराम्:crowned); वृषारूढां (vṛṣārūḍhāṁ) – mounted on a bull (वृष:bull | आरूढां:mounted); शूलधरां (śūladharāṁ) – bearing a trident (शूल:trident | धरां:bearing); शैलपुत्रीं (śailaputrīṁ) – daughter of the mountain; यशस्विनीम् (yaśasvinīm) – glorious;

contentBlocks.original:
देवी ब्रह्मचारिणी ।
दधाना करपद्माभ्यामक्षमालाकमण्डलू ।
देवी प्रसीदतु मयि ब्रह्मचारिण्यनुत्तमा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
devī brahmacāriṇī .
dadhānā karapadmābhyāmakṣamālākamaṇḍalū .
devī prasīdatu mayi brahmacāriṇyanuttamā ..
contentBlocks.translation: Brahmacāriṇī — the second Durgā, the ascetic seeker of Brahman. May the incomparable goddess Brahmacāriṇī be gracious to me (devī prasīdatu mayi brahmacāriṇī anuttamā): she who holds in her two lotus-hands the rosary of prayer beads (akṣamālā) and the sacred water-pot (kamaṇḍalu) — the goddess of devoted austerity and spiritual quest.
contentBlocks.meanings: देवी (devī) – goddess; ब्रह्मचारिणी (brahmacāriṇī) – seeker of Brahman; दधाना (dadhānā) – holding; करपद्माभ्यामक्षमालाकमण्डलू (karapadmābhyām akṣamālā kamaṇḍalū) – with rosary and water pot in lotus hands (करपद्माभ्याम्:with lotus hands | अक्षमाला:rosary | कमण्डलू:water pot); देवी (devī) – goddess; प्रसीदतु (prasīdatu) – may she be pleased; मयि (mayi) – upon me; ब्रह्मचारिण्यनुत्तमा (brahmacāriṇyanuttamā) – supreme seeker of Brahman (ब्रह्मचारिण्य:seeker of Brahman | अनुत्तमा:supreme);

contentBlocks.original:
देवी चन्द्रघण्टेति ।
पिण्डजप्रवरारूढा चण्डकोपास्त्रकैर्युता ।
प्रसादं तनुते मह्यं चन्द्रघण्टेति विश्रुता ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
devī candraghaṇṭeti .
piṇḍajapravarārūḍhā caṇḍakopāstrakairyutā .
prasādaṃ tanute mahyaṃ candraghaṇṭeti viśrutā ..
contentBlocks.translation: Candraghaṇṭā — the third Durgā, the moon-bell goddess. The goddess known as Candraghaṇṭā (candrāghaṇṭēti viśrutā) — mounted on the tiger (piṇḍajapravaraārūḍhā), armed with weapons of fierce power (caṇḍakopāstrakairyutā) — extends her grace to me (prasādaṃ tanute mahyam). May she shower her blessings!
contentBlocks.meanings: देवी (devī) – goddess; चन्द्रघण्टेति (candragaṇṭeti) – named Candraghaṇṭā (चन्द्र:moon | घण्टा:bell | इति:thus); पिण्डजप्रवरारूढा (piṇḍajapravarārūḍhā) – mounted on the best of bodies (पिण्डज:body | प्रवर:best | आरूढा:mounted); चण्डकोपास्त्रकैर्युता (caṇḍakopāstrakairyutā) – equipped with fierce weapons (चण्ड:fierce | कोप:anger | अस्त्र:weapon | कैः:with | युता:equipped); प्रसादं (prasādaṁ) – grace; तनुते (tanute) – extends; मह्यं (mahyaṁ) – to me; चन्द्रघण्टेति (candragaṇṭeti) – named Candraghaṇṭā (चन्द्र:moon | घण्टा:bell | इति:thus); विश्रुता (viśrutā) – renowned;

contentBlocks.original:
देवी कूष्मांडा ।
सुरासम्पूर्णकलशं रुधिराप्लुतमेव च ।
दधाना हस्तपद्माभ्यां कूष्माण्डा शुभदास्तु मे ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
devī kūṣmāṃḍā .
surāsampūrṇakalaśaṃ rudhirāplutameva ca .
dadhānā hastapadmābhyāṃ kūṣmāṇḍā śubhadāstu me ..
contentBlocks.translation: Kūṣmāṇḍā — the fourth Durgā, who created the cosmos with her smile. May Kūṣmāṇḍā bestow all that is auspicious upon me (kūṣmāṇḍā śubhadāstu me): she who holds in her two lotus-hands a vessel brimming with divine nectar (surāsampūrṇakalaśam) and another dripping with the blood of demons (rudhirāplutam) — the cosmic mother whose laughter brought creation.
contentBlocks.meanings: देवी (devī) – goddess; कूष्मांडा (kūṣmāṇḍā) – Kūṣmāṇḍā (a goddess); सुरासम्पूर्णकलशं (surāsampūrṇakalaśaṁ) – pot full of nectar (सुरा:nectar | सम्पूर्ण:full | कलशं:pot); रुधिराप्लुतमेव (rudhirāplutameva) – immersed in blood (रुधिर:blood | आप्लुत:immersed | एव:indeed); च (ca) – and; दधाना (dadhānā) – holding; हस्तपद्माभ्यां (hastapadmābhyāṁ) – with lotus-like hands (हस्त:hand | पद्माभ्यां:with lotus); कूष्माण्डा (kūṣmāṇḍā) – Kūṣmāṇḍā (a goddess); शुभदास्तु (śubhadāstu) – may she grant auspiciousness (शुभदा:granting auspiciousness | अस्तु:may be); मे (me) – to me;

contentBlocks.original:
देवीस्कन्दमाता ।
सिंहासनगता नित्यं पद्माश्रितकरद्वया ।
शुभदास्तु सदा देवी स्कन्दमाता यशस्विनी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
devīskandamātā .
siṃhāsanagatā nityaṃ padmāśritakaradvayā .
śubhadāstu sadā devī skandamātā yaśasvinī ..
contentBlocks.translation: Skandamātā — the fifth Durgā, the mother of Skanda. May the glorious goddess Skandamātā always bestow auspiciousness upon me (śubhadāstu sadā devī skandamātā yaśasvinī): she who is always seated upon her lion-throne (siṃhāsanagatā nityam), with her two hands resting upon lotus flowers (padmāśritakaradvayā) as she lovingly holds the infant Skanda.
contentBlocks.meanings: देवीस्कन्दमाता (devīskandamātā) – Mother of Skanda (देवी:goddess | स्कन्दमाता:Mother of Skanda); सिंहासनगता (siṃhāsanagatā) – seated on a throne (सिंहासन:throne | गता:gone to); नित्यं (nityaṃ) – always; पद्माश्रितकरद्वया (padmāśritakaradvayā) – holding lotus in two hands (पद्म:lotus | आश्रित:holding | कर:hand | द्वया:two); शुभदास्तु (śubhadāstu) – may she grant auspiciousness (शुभदा:granting auspiciousness | अस्तु:may be); सदा (sadā) – always; देवी (devī) – goddess; स्कन्दमाता (skandamātā) – Mother of Skanda (स्कन्द:Skanda | माता:mother); यशस्विनी (yaśasvinī) – glorious;

contentBlocks.original:
देवीकात्यायनी ।
चन्द्रहासोज्ज्वलकरा शार्दूलवरवाहना ।
कात्यायनी शुभं दद्यादेवी दानवघातिनी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
devīkātyāyanī .
candrahāsojjvalakarā śārdūlavaravāhanā .
kātyāyanī śubhaṃ dadyādevī dānavaghātinī ..
contentBlocks.translation: Kātyāyanī — the sixth Durgā, born to the sage Kātyāyana. May goddess Kātyāyanī, slayer of all demons (dānavaghātinī), bestow auspiciousness upon me (kātyāyanī śubhaṃ dadyāt): she whose hands blaze with the bright moon-sword (candrahāsojjvalakarā), who rides upon the excellent tiger (śārdūlavaravāhanā).
contentBlocks.meanings: देवीकात्यायनी (devīkātyāyanī) – Goddess Katyayani (देवी:goddess | कात्यायनी:Katyayani); चन्द्रहासोज्ज्वलकरा (candrahāsojjvalakarā) – shining sword in hand (चन्द्रहास:sword | उज्ज्वल:shining | करा:hand); शार्दूलवरवाहना (śārdūlavaravāhanā) – tiger as vehicle (शार्दूल:tiger | वर:excellent | वाहना:vehicle); कात्यायनी (kātyāyanī) – Katyayani; शुभं (śubhaṁ) – auspiciousness; दद्यादेवी (dadyādevī) – may the goddess give (दद्यात्:may give | देवी:goddess); दानवघातिनी (dānavaghātinī) – destroyer of demons (दानव:demon | घातिनी:destroyer);

contentBlocks.original:
देवीकालरात्रि ।
एकवेणी जपाकर्णपूरा नग्ना खरास्थिता ।
लम्बोष्ठी कर्णिकाकर्णी तैलाभ्यक्तशरीरिणी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
devīkālarātri .
ekaveṇī japākarṇapūrā nagnā kharāsthitā .
lamboṣṭhī karṇikākarṇī tailābhyaktaśarīriṇī ..
contentBlocks.translation: Kālarātri — the seventh Durgā, the dark night of time. She has a single braid of hair; her ears are adorned with the red japā flower; she stands naked, mounted on the donkey (kharāsthitā); her lips are large, her earrings are lotus-seed shaped; her body is anointed with oil — this fierce form of the goddess is her seventh aspect.
contentBlocks.meanings: देवीकालरात्रि (devīkālarātri) – Goddess Kalaratri (देवी:goddess | कालरात्रि:Kalaratri); एकवेणी (ekaveṇī) – single braid; जपाकर्णपूरा (japākarṇapūrā) – adorned with hibiscus; नग्ना (nagnā) – naked; खरास्थिता (kharāsthitā) – seated on a donkey; लम्बोष्ठी (lamboṣṭhī) – long-lipped; कर्णिकाकर्णी (karṇikākarṇī) – ear to ear; तैलाभ्यक्तशरीरिणी (tailābhyaktaśarīriṇī) – body smeared with oil (तैल:oil | अभ्यक्त:smeared | शरीरिणी:having a body);

contentBlocks.translation: Kālarātri continued — adorned with creepers of iron thorns wound around her raised left foot (vāmapādollasallohalatākaṇṭakabhūṣaṇā); her banner rises from her head; dark-complexioned (kṛṣṇā) — Kālarātri, the awesome and terrifying seventh goddess, destroyer of darkness and evil (bhayaṃkarī).
contentBlocks.meanings: वामपादोल्लसल्लोहलताकण्टकभूषणा (vāmapādollasallohalatākaṇṭakabhūṣaṇā) – adorned with iron thorn creepers (वामपाद:left foot | उल्लसत्:shining | लोहलता:iron creepers | कण्टक:thorn | भूषणा:adorned); वर्धन्मूर्धध्वजा (vardhanmūrdhadhvajā) – with a growing head flag (वर्धन्:growing | मूर्ध:head | ध्वजा:flag); कृष्णा (kṛṣṇā) – dark one; कालरात्रिर्भयङ्करी (kālarātribhayaṅkarī) – fearsome as the dark night (कालरात्रि:dark night | भयङ्करी:fearsome);

contentBlocks.original:
देवीमहागौरी ।
श्वेते वृषे समारूढा श्वेताम्बरधरा शुचिः ।
महागौरी शुभं दद्यान्महादेवप्रमोददा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
devīmahāgaurī .
śvete vṛṣe samārūḍhā śvetāmbaradharā śuciḥ .
mahāgaurī śubhaṃ dadyānmahādevapramodadā ..
contentBlocks.translation: Mahāgaurī — the eighth Durgā, the goddess of supreme radiance. May Mahāgaurī — who fills Mahādeva with joy (mahādevapramodadā) — bestow all that is auspicious upon me (mahāgaurī śubhaṃ dadyāt): she who is mounted on the white bull (śvete vṛṣe samārūḍhā), wearing pure white garments (śvetāmbaradharā), immaculate and luminous (śuciḥ).
contentBlocks.meanings: देवीमहागौरी (devīmahāgaurī) – Goddess Mahagauri (देवी:goddess | महागौरी:Mahagauri); श्वेते (śvete) – white; वृषे (vṛṣe) – bull; समारूढा (samārūḍhā) – mounted; श्वेताम्बरधरा (śvetāmbaradharā) – wearing white clothes (श्वेत:white | अम्बर:clothes | धरा:wearing); शुचिः (śuciḥ) – pure; महागौरी (mahāgaurī) – Mahagauri; शुभं (śubhaṁ) – auspicious; दद्यान्महादेवप्रमोददा (dadyānmahādevapramodadā) – giver of joy to Mahadeva (दद्यात्:giver | महादेव:Mahadeva | प्रमोददा:giver of joy);

contentBlocks.original:
देवीसिद्धिदात्रि ।
सिद्धगन्धर्वयक्षाद्यैरसुरैरमरैरपि ।
सेव्यमाना सदा भूयात् सिद्धिदा सिद्धिदायिनी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
devīsiddhidātri .
siddhagandharvayakṣādyairasurairamarairapi .
sevyamānā sadā bhūyāt siddhidā siddhidāyinī ..
contentBlocks.translation: Siddhidātri — the ninth and final Durgā, granter of all perfections. May Siddhidātri — who always grants perfections (siddhidā siddhidāyinī) — ever be gracious: she who is always served and worshipped by Siddhas, Gandharvas, Yakṣas and others, by demons (asuras) and by the immortal gods (amaras) alike (siddhagandharvayakṣādyairasurairamarairapi sēvyamānā sadā).
contentBlocks.meanings: देवीसिद्धिदात्रि (devīsiddhidātri) – goddess bestower of success (देवी:goddess | सिद्धिदात्रि:bestower of success); सिद्धगन्धर्वयक्षाद्यैरसुरैरमरैरपि (siddhagandharvayakṣādyairasurairamarairapi) – by Siddhas, Gandharvas, Yakshas, etc. (सिद्ध:Siddhas | गन्धर्व:Gandharvas | यक्ष:Yakshas | आद्यैः:etc. | असुरैः:by Asuras | अमरैः:by immortals | अपि:also); सेव्यमाना (sevyamānā) – being served; सदा (sadā) – always; भूयात् (bhūyāt) – may she be; सिद्धिदा (siddhidā) – giver of success; सिद्धिदायिनी (siddhidāyinī) – bestower of success;

contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Navadurgā Stotram — the hymn to the nine divine aspects of Goddess Durgā.
contentBlocks.meanings: इति (iti) – thus; नवदुर्गास्तोत्रं (navadurgāstotraṁ) – hymn to Navadurga (नवदुर्गा:Navadurga | स्तोत्रं:hymn); सम्पूर्णम् (sampūrṇam) – complete;
